leadText: The Cherish Ball (Japanese: プレシャスボール Precious Ball) is a type of Poké Ball introduced in Generation IV. Event Pokémon are often contained in a Cherish Ball.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Manual activation [ edit | edit source ] The Cherish Ball cannot be legitimately obtained as an item, so it cannot normally be used. If it were to be obtained, when used from the Bag in a wild encounter, it would attempt to catch the wild Pokémon with a catch rate modifier of 1×. As a Pokémon's Poké Ball [ edit | edit source ] From Generation VI onward, bred Pokémon inherit their Poké Ball from their parents. However, any offspring that would inherit a Cherish Ball, Master Ball , or Strange Ball will instead be contained in a standard Poké Ball .

sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description D P Pt HG SS B W B2 W2 A quite rare Poké Ball that has been specially crafted to commemorate an occasion of some sort. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA A quite rare Poké Ball that has been crafted in order to commemorate a special occasion of some sort. sections.title: In the core series games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 Price 1.2 Effect 1.2.1 Manual activation 1.2.2 As a Pokémon's Poké Ball 1.3 Description 1.4 Acquisition 1.5 NPC usage 1.5.1 Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] From Generation IV onward, event Pokémon are usually distributed inside a Cherish Ball, unless they are distributed as Eggs . This is the only way to obtain a Pokémon in a Cherish Ball. Price [ edit | edit source ] Games Cost Sell price D P Pt HG SS B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh S V ZA N/A N/A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Manual activation [ edit | edit source ] The Cherish Ball cannot be legitimately obtained as an item, so it cannot normally be used. If it were to be obtained, when used from the Bag in a wild encounter, it would attempt to catch the wild Pokémon with a catch rate modifier of 1×. As a Pokémon's Poké Ball [ edit | edit source ] From Generation VI onward, bred Pokémon inherit their Poké Ball from their parents. However, any offspring that would inherit a Cherish Ball, Master Ball , or Strange Ball will instead be contained in a standard Poké Ball . Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description D P Pt HG SS B W B2 W2 A quite rare Poké Ball that has been specially crafted to commemorate an occasion of some sort. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA A quite rare Poké Ball that has been crafted in order to commemorate a special occasion of some sort. S V ZA A quite rare Poké Ball made to commemorate a special occasion of some sort. Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] The Cherish Ball cannot be obtained in any game. NPC usage [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl [ edit | edit source ] Ace Trainer Deanna keeps her Tropius in a Cherish Ball. This is a reference to Shokotan , who Deanna shares her Japanese name with. Shokotan's Tropius was the first event Pokémon distributed in a Cherish Ball.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Platinum , if the player hacks Cherish Balls into the game and uses one to catch the in-game Regigigas , the game considers it having been obtained from an event and thus unlocks the Rock Peak , Iceberg , and Iron Ruins . Despite the fact that a hatched Pokémon can never be contained in a Cherish Ball, six Eggs containing Pokémon that hatch with Cherish Balls were distributed in Japan and South Korea in 2017. The Cherish Ball and Park Ball are the only Poké Balls introduced in the core series games prior to Pokémon Legends: Arceus that cannot be obtained in Pokémon Sword and Shield . Pecharunt is the only Mythical Pokémon to have never been made available in a Cherish Ball. In the Mochi Mayhem event where it is first catchable, the player can choose whichever Ball they want to keep it in, as Pecharunt's battle is scripted so that every Poké Ball is guaranteed to catch it.
